rulings.Case Title: AMY PITNER VS. ROBERT RASNUSSEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents From Pltf In Compliance With Pltf'S Agreement To Produce Documents Responsive To Req. For Production Of Documents, Set 2 Propounded; Req. For Sanctions Against Pltf And Pltf'S Counsel Of Record (In The Amount Of $1121.00)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 23, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANT AVIS BUDGET CAR RENTAL, LLC'S (SUED AS AVIS RENT A CAR SYSTEM, LLC) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Plaintiff In Compliance With Plaintiff's Agreement To Produce Documents Responsive To Request For Production Of Documents, Set 2 Propounded; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Plaintiff's Counsel Of Record (In The Amount Of $1121.00) Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Denied. Request for sanctions denied. Moving party failed to comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1435. Additionally, it appears from the papers that motion is now moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: LANA BONDAR VS. IGOR ELGART, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS D& et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Compliance
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 23, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT D & A BUILDERS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Compliance Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Denied. The witnesses were not served with sufficient time to appear. No sanctions are ordered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CHEUNG VS. AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 23, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 4, PLAINTIFF DAVID CHEUNG'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Granted. The production of reserves information is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. See Lipton v. Superior Court (1996) 48 Cal. App. 4th 1599. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CIAPPONI et al VS. SIMUS, INC. DBA SIMAYOF DIAMOND CUTTERS & JEWELERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 23, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT MICKY TAL's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiffs have pled facts sufficient to allege the causes of action for conversion and fraud against Mr. Tal. "One who assumes to act as an agent is responsible to third persons as a principal for his acts in the course of his agency . . . [w]hen his acts are wrongful in their nature." (Civ. Code § 2343(3).) =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID PERIERA VS. JAMES GONZALEZ M.D. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 23, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff pled sufficient facts to put Defendant Dignity Health dba Mercy Medical Center Redding on notice of the claims against it and allow it to begin preparing a defense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLINE ARMITAGE VS. EMMETT HEGARTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 23, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTs PEAK REALTY GROUP, INC. and JIM KEIGHRAN's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied. Plaintiff has sufficiently plead facts to support a claim for punitive damages. The Court does not consider extrinsic evidence cited in reply to the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLINE ARMITAGE VS. EMMETT HEGARTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 23, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT EMMETT HEGARTY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Hearing required. The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the opposition to this motion as required by Local Rule 2.6B. If Plaintiff provides the opposition by 9:00 a.m. on January 23, 2013, the Court will hear the motion on the merits on January 23. Otherwise the matter will be continued. If possible, Plaintiff should email a copy of the opposition to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CURATOLO VS. JANE GRONA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Anthony Curatolo'S Motion To Compel Defendant'S Compliance With Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 23, 2013, Line 35, Plaintiff Anthony Curatolo'S Motion To Compel Defendant'S Compliance With Documents. Denied. Though titled a Motion to Compel Compliance, this matter is a motion to compel a further response. Therefore, the motion is untimely per CCP § 2031.310. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
